user_query:
I just binged a chunk of the MCU in one massive run and want a grounded, reusable summary I can share. Using only the provided files, please:

1) Extract the catalog data from input/marvel_catalog.html (it has a single table of titles) and compute total_runtime_minutes for each title (for movies: Runtime Minutes; for shows: Episodes × Avg Episode Minutes). Output a timeline CSV sorted by Timeline Index ascending to outputs/mcu_timeline.csv with columns: timeline_index,title,type,phase,release_date,total_runtime_minutes.

2) Aggregate per-phase stats into outputs/phase_stats.json. For each phase present an object with: phase, titles (count), movies (count), shows (count), total_runtime_minutes.

3) Validate my binge log in input/watch_log.jsonl by comparing the observed duration (finished_at − started_at, in minutes) for each title against the expected total_runtime_minutes from the catalog. Create outputs/validation_report.json with:
   - an array of per-title entries: { title, expected_runtime_minutes, observed_minutes, difference_minutes, percent_diff, status }, where status is "ok" if |percent_diff| ≤ 5, else "mismatch";
   - a summary object: { total_titles, ok_count, mismatch_count, tolerance_percent: 5 }.

4) Rewrite input/rough_note.txt into a concise, friendly note under 200 words that includes: (a) the total number of titles, (b) total runtime across all titles in minutes and in hours rounded to one decimal, (c) the top 3 longest titles by total_runtime_minutes, and (d) the story order as a single line exactly in this form:
   Captain America: The First Avenger -> Captain Marvel -> Iron Man -> The Avengers -> WandaVision -> The Falcon and the Winter Soldier
   Save this to outputs/note_cleaned.txt.

Package the logic into a single runnable command-line script at scripts/mcu_report.py. Running:

  python scripts/mcu_report.py input/marvel_catalog.html input/watch_log.jsonl input/rough_note.txt outputs/

must generate all four outputs above. Please run the script to produce the files under outputs/ so I can inspect them. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
